text_blocks.text: Association football is played in accordance
with a set of rules known as the Laws of the
Game. The game is played using a spherical ball
known as the football (or soccer ball). Two teams
of eleven players each compete to get the ball into
the other team's goal (between the posts and under
the bar), thereby scoring a goal. The team that
has scored more goals at the end of the game is
the winner; if both teams have scored an equal
number of goals then the game is a draw. Each
team is led by a captain who has only one
official responsibility as mandated by the Laws of
the Game: to be involved in the coin toss prior to
